text:
University College Hospital 

National Hospital for Neurology and Neurosurgery 

Clinic Date: 02 October 2025 

Dr Helen Cartwright  
Oakview Medical Practice  
12 Oakview Lane, Highbury, London N5 2QL  

Dear Dr Helen Cartwright,  

Daniel Mercer, DOB: 22-07-1987, Hospital No: U918274 NHS No. 6042198735  
Flat 3, 27 Ashfield Road, London, N4 1TR 

Summary/ Diagnoses: History of seizures following viral encephalitis in 2018/ongoing memory and word-finding difficulties/mild anxiety symptoms/vitamin D insufficiency/uses smartphone reminders for medication adherence/non-smoker/does not drink alcohol. 

Medication: Lamotrigine 150mg twice daily.  
Sertraline 50mg once daily.  
Cholecalciferol 1000 units once daily.  
Omeprazole 20mg once daily as required for dyspepsia.  

I reviewed Daniel Mercer in clinic today, accompanied by his partner who helps keep a simple calendar record of events. He reports better sleep since starting a regular evening routine and has returned to part-time work as a bicycle mechanic. He continues to experience brief, stereotyped episodes of tingling rising from the left hand into the forearm with preserved awareness, without motor features or loss of speech. He notes these occur reliably under stress and with missed meals. He has not reported any episodes with loss of awareness, convulsions or injuries since our last review. 

Seizures: Based on the calendar record and patient description, the current pattern is focal sensory once every two days. Each episode lasts under a minute, does not generalise, and resolves spontaneously without a post-event headache or confusion. No nocturnal events reported. He has not required rescue medication. 

Examination: Neurological examination today was stable with normal cranial nerves, full power in all limbs and normal coordination. Gait narrow-based and steady. No new focal deficits. 

Investigations: Last routine bloods (U&E, LFTs, FBC) in July 2025 were within reference limits. Lamotrigine level not measured. MRI brain (2019) showed post-encephalitic change in right temporal region without mass effect. 

Plan: 
- Continue lamotrigine at current dose; no immediate dose change given brief, non-disabling events and preserved awareness.  
- Request lamotrigine level with next bloods to aid future optimisation; practice phlebotomy acceptable.  
- Provide written advice on sleep regularity, hydration and avoiding prolonged fasting, as these appear to precipitate episodes.  
- Patient to maintain calendar record and bring to next appointment; consider ambulatory EEG if frequency pattern changes or events evolve.  
- Safety advice reinforced for cycling and workshop tasks, including stepping away from machinery at onset of symptoms.  
- Review in 6 months, sooner if events increase in duration, occur in clusters, or if any loss of awareness develops.  

Yours sincerely

entities.text: current pattern is focal sensory once every two days
focal sensory once every two days

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter explicitly states the current seizure pattern: 'focal sensory once every two days.' This specifies a frequency of 1 seizure per 2 days. No other seizure types with higher frequency are reported. There are no losses of awareness or generalized events since last review, but focal sensory events continue with this frequency. Therefore, normalize 'once every two days' to '1 per 2 day.'
seizure_frequency_number: current pattern is focal sensory once every two days
reference: focal sensory once every two days
